applications.name: Claude
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to Claude as a remote MCP server with OAuth.
setupSteps: Open Claude settings and go to Customize > Connectors.
Click +, choose Add custom connector, and name it Breakcold.
Paste the MCP server URL, add the connector, then connect or sign in when Claude asks.
In a chat, click + > Connectors and enable Breakcold for the conversation.

applications.name: ChatGPT
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to ChatGPT apps or custom MCP apps.
setupSteps: Open ChatGPT settings and go to Apps.
Add or enable the Breakcold app for your workspace.
Use the MCP server URL when ChatGPT asks for the remote MCP endpoint.
Connect the app, then call Breakcold from the app picker or with an @ mention.
applications.setupNote: Workspace admins may need to enable custom apps before members can connect Breakcold.

applications.name: Codex
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to Codex for agent workflows that need CRM context.
setupSteps: Add the Breakcold MCP server with `codex mcp add breakcold --url https://http.us.breakcold.com/mcp/v1`.
Authorize Codex with Breakcold using `codex mcp login breakcold` when OAuth is available.
Run `codex mcp list` to verify the server is configured.
Use the Europe MCP endpoint instead if your Breakcold workspace is hosted in Europe.
applications.setupNote: For bearer-token access, configure `bearer_token_env_var = "BREAKCOLD_MCP_TOKEN"` in the Breakcold MCP server entry in your Codex config.

applications.name: TypingMind
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to TypingMind as a remote MCP server.
setupSteps: Open TypingMind and go to the MCP Store or MCP settings.
Choose to add a remote MCP server and paste the MCP server URL.
When TypingMind asks for a connector, use TypingMind Cloud or your private connector.
Authenticate with Breakcold or provide a bearer key if TypingMind asks for an API key.

applications.name: LM Studio
applications.description: Use Breakcold tools from local models in LM Studio chat.
setupSteps: Open LM Studio and switch to the Program tab.
Click Install > Edit mcp.json.
Add Breakcold as a remote MCP server using the MCP server URL.
If prompted for headers, set Authorization to Bearer followed by a Breakcold bearer key.

applications.name: OpenClaw
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to OpenClaw as a remote MCP server.
setupSteps: Add Breakcold to OpenClaw's MCP server registry with openclaw mcp set.
Use the MCP server URL for your Breakcold workspace region.
Set transport to streamable-http.
Use an Authorization bearer header with a Breakcold bearer key.
applications.setupNote: OpenClaw stores MCP server definitions for runtimes that consume MCP servers, so keep the Breakcold bearer key scoped to the minimum tools the agent needs.

applications.name: Hermes
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to Hermes as a remote MCP server.
setupSteps: Open your Hermes MCP config.
Add Breakcold under mcp_servers with the MCP server URL for your workspace region.
Set auth to oauth for user sign-in.
Restart Hermes or run /reload-mcp after saving the config.
applications.setupNote: For bearer-token fallback, set an Authorization bearer header with a narrow Breakcold bearer key and only expose the tools the Hermes agent needs.

applications.name: LibreChat
applications.description: Connect Breakcold to LibreChat workspaces that have MCP enabled.
setupSteps: Open the MCP settings panel from LibreChat.
Click + to add a server, name it Breakcold, and choose Streamable HTTP.
Paste the MCP server URL.
Use API key authentication with Bearer format if LibreChat asks for credentials.

applications.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
applications.description: Connect Breakcold through Microsoft 365 Copilot custom connectors.
setupSteps: Ask a Microsoft 365 admin to open the admin center and go to Copilot > Connectors.
Create a custom connector and choose Connect to MCP server.
Use the MCP server URL as the connector base URL.
Complete the OAuth setup so users can connect with their own Breakcold access.
applications.setupNote: Microsoft custom federated connectors currently focus on read-only MCP tools, so validate the Breakcold tool set before publishing it broadly.
